Overview

Address
5801 Bryant Irvin Rd, Fort Worth, TX 76132
Phone
8173463030
Certified beds
210
Avg daily residents
169 (81% of beds filled)

Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1996-07-30
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.47 3.69 3.63 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.43 0.65 0.45 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.11 0.82 1.16 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.92 2.23 2.01 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.54 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.17
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.31
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.31
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
51.1%
annual
RN turnover
42.9%
annual
Administrators departed
3
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-08-21 10 6 4 169 1 169
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-25 28 19 23 522 1 522
Total weighted health score
257.3
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (41)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

37 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
4 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2026-01-29 —
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2025-08-21 2025-08-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-08-21 2025-08-22
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-08-21 2025-08-22
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2025-08-21 2025-08-22
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2025-08-21 2025-08-22
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-08-21 2025-08-22
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2025-07-10 2025-07-11
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. K 2025-07-10 2025-07-11
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-06-17 2025-02-03
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-01-31 2024-08-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. K 2025-01-31 2025-02-01
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. K 2024-09-17 2024-09-18
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. K 2024-09-17 2024-09-18
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0840 Employ or obtain outside professional resources to provide services in the nursing home when the facility does not employ a qualified professional to furnish a required service. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0914 Provide bedrooms that don't allow residents to see each other when privacy is needed. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-23
0563 Honor the resident's right to receive visitors of his or her choosing, at the time of his or her choosing. E 2024-06-04 2024-06-10
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-06-04 2024-06-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-05-10 2024-05-11
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-02-20 2024-02-21
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2023-09-15 2023-09-18
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-06-08 2023-06-09
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. E 2023-06-08 2023-06-09
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2023-06-08 2023-06-09

  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity E 0761.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0761 — survey_date 2024-02-20 0761.survey_date
Deficiencies 0761 — tag 0761 0761.tag
Deficiencies 0804 — correction_date 2023-06-09 0804.correction_date
Deficiencies 0804 — description Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. 0804.description
Deficiencies 0804 — scope_severity E 0804.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0804 — survey_date 2023-06-08 0804.survey_date
Deficiencies 0804 — tag 0804 0804.tag
Deficiencies 0812 — correction_date 2024-08-23 0812.correction_date
Deficiencies 0812 — description Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. 0812.description
Deficiencies 0812 — scope_severity F 0812.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0812 — survey_date 2024-07-25 0812.survey_date
Deficiencies 0812 — tag 0812 0812.tag
Deficiencies 0838 — correction_date 2024-08-23 0838.correction_date
Deficiencies 0838 — description Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. 0838.description
Deficiencies 0838 — scope_severity D 0838.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0838 — survey_date 2024-07-25 0838.survey_date
Deficiencies 0838 — tag 0838 0838.tag
Deficiencies 0840 — correction_date 2024-08-23 0840.correction_date
Deficiencies 0840 — description Employ or obtain outside professional resources to provide services in the nursing home when the facility does not employ a qualified professional to furnish a required service. 0840.description
Deficiencies 0840 — scope_severity D 0840.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0840 — survey_date 2024-07-25 0840.survey_date
Deficiencies 0840 — tag 0840 0840.tag
Deficiencies 0842 — correction_date 2024-06-10 0842.correction_date
Deficiencies 0842 — correction_date 2024-08-23 0842.correction_date
Deficiencies 0842 — description Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. 0842.description
Deficiencies 0842 — description Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. 0842.description

Deficiencies 0842 — tag 0842 0842.tag

Deficiencies 0849 — description Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. 0849.description
Deficiencies 0849 — scope_severity D 0849.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0849 — survey_date 2024-07-25 0849.survey_date
